processes.name: Month-end bank reconciliation
processes.description: Matching bank transactions to ledger entries, chasing discrepancies, and posting adjustments.
processes.explanation: AI auto-matches 80-90% of transactions and flags only genuine discrepancies. What used to take a full day per client compresses to a quick review.

processes.name: Client onboarding paperwork
processes.description: Collecting engagement letters, AML checks, proof of identity, authorisations, and setting up the client in practice management software.
processes.explanation: AI pre-populates forms from Companies House data, runs AML checks automatically, and chases missing documents via email — cutting onboarding from days to hours.

processes.name: VAT return preparation
processes.description: Pulling transactions, categorising by VAT rate, running the calculation, generating the return, and submitting to HMRC via MTD.
processes.explanation: AI categorises transactions using learned patterns from prior returns, flags anomalies, and drafts the return for review. Turns a half-day job into a 20-minute check.

processes.name: Chasing outstanding client invoices
processes.description: Tracking unpaid invoices, sending reminders at 30/60/90 days, escalating overdue accounts, and updating the aged debtors report.
processes.explanation: Automated reminder sequences with personalised tone. AI prioritises by amount and relationship risk. Partners only get involved when it actually matters.

processes.name: Annual accounts preparation
processes.description: Compiling trial balance, making year-end adjustments, preparing statutory accounts, and generating the filing package for Companies House.
processes.explanation: AI drafts standard notes, identifies missing adjustments by comparing to prior year, and flags areas needing partner review. The heavy lifting shifts from assembly to judgment.

processes.name: Tax return data gathering
processes.description: Requesting P60s, dividend vouchers, rental income figures, and pension contributions from clients — often multiple chase cycles.
processes.explanation: AI sends structured data requests to clients, tracks what's outstanding, auto-extracts figures from uploaded documents, and populates the return template.

processes.name: Payroll processing
processes.description: Running monthly payroll: calculating gross-to-net, applying tax codes, generating payslips, filing RTI submissions to HMRC.
processes.explanation: AI handles the routine calculation and filing. Human involvement shifts to exceptions — new starters, statutory payments, and HMRC queries.

processes.name: Client email triage and response
processes.description: Reading, categorising, and responding to the daily flow of client queries about deadlines, document requests, and general questions.
processes.explanation: AI drafts responses to common queries using firm templates and client context. Unusual questions get flagged for human attention with suggested answers.

processes.name: Expense categorisation
processes.description: Reviewing and coding client expense receipts and bank transactions to the correct nominal codes in the accounting software.
processes.explanation: AI learns your coding patterns and auto-categorises with high accuracy. Exceptions get flagged. Bulk processing replaces line-by-line review.

processes.name: Management reporting for clients
processes.description: Producing monthly or quarterly management accounts, commentary, and KPI dashboards for advisory clients.
processes.explanation: AI generates first-draft commentary by comparing current period to prior periods and highlighting significant movements. Accountants add insight instead of assembling data.

processes.name: Document review and due diligence
processes.description: Reviewing large volumes of documents during transactions or disputes to identify relevant provisions or risks.
processes.explanation: AI scans thousands of pages in minutes, flags relevant clauses, identifies risk patterns, and produces a structured summary. Lawyers focus on judgment calls, not reading.

processes.name: Client intake and conflict checks
processes.description: Running new client details against existing records for conflicts of interest, collecting KYC/AML documentation, and setting up the matter.
processes.explanation: AI runs conflict checks instantly across all systems, auto-populates intake forms from Companies House and public records, and triggers KYC workflows automatically.

processes.name: Time recording and narrative entry
processes.description: Lawyers recording their time throughout the day — entering descriptions of work done and allocating to the right matter code.
processes.explanation: AI captures work activity passively (emails sent, documents edited, calls made) and drafts time entries for lawyer approval. Recovers the 15-20% of billable time that typically goes unrecorded.

processes.name: First-draft contract assembly
processes.description: Creating initial drafts of standard agreements (NDAs, employment contracts, shareholder agreements) by selecting clauses from precedent banks.
processes.explanation: AI assembles first drafts from your precedent library using deal-specific parameters. Lawyers move straight to review and negotiation instead of cut-and-paste.

processes.name: Legal research and case analysis
processes.description: Researching relevant legislation, case law, and regulatory guidance to support advice or build arguments.
processes.explanation: AI searches across legal databases, summarises relevant authorities, and identifies supporting or conflicting precedent. Paralegals produce research memos in hours, not days.

processes.name: Client correspondence and updates
processes.description: Writing and sending status updates, next-steps letters, and general correspondence to clients on ongoing matters.
processes.explanation: AI drafts correspondence using matter context and firm tone. Fee earners review and personalise rather than composing from scratch.

processes.name: Invoice preparation and billing
processes.description: Reviewing WIP, writing billing narratives, applying agreed fee arrangements, and generating the final bill.
processes.explanation: AI drafts billing narratives from time entries, applies fee caps or fixed-fee arrangements automatically, and flags WIP that's been unbilled too long.

processes.name: Court deadline and compliance tracking
processes.description: Managing limitation dates, filing deadlines, hearing dates, and compliance milestones across all active matters.
processes.explanation: AI monitors all deadlines across the practice, sends escalating reminders, and flags clashes or risks. Nothing gets missed because someone was on holiday.

processes.name: Disclosure and document bundling
processes.description: Preparing document bundles for litigation — paginating, indexing, redacting privileged content, and ensuring completeness.
processes.explanation: AI auto-indexes, paginates, and cross-references documents. Identifies potentially privileged material for review. Reduces a week-long bundling exercise to a day.

processes.name: Know Your Client (KYC) renewals
processes.description: Re-verifying existing client identity documents, checking sanctions lists, and updating records for AML compliance.
processes.explanation: AI runs batch checks against sanctions and PEP databases, identifies expired documentation, and sends renewal requests automatically. Compliance officers review exceptions only.

processes.name: CV screening and shortlisting
processes.description: Reading through hundreds of CVs per role to identify candidates who meet the job specification, then producing a shortlist.
processes.explanation: AI scores CVs against the job spec instantly, explains why each candidate does or doesn't fit, and produces a ranked shortlist. Recruiters spend time on the best candidates, not all candidates.

processes.name: Candidate sourcing on job boards and LinkedIn
processes.description: Searching job boards, LinkedIn, and internal databases to find passive candidates, then crafting personalised outreach messages.
processes.explanation: AI searches across multiple platforms simultaneously, identifies matching candidates, and drafts personalised outreach messages using the candidate's profile and role context.

processes.name: Interview scheduling and coordination
processes.description: Coordinating diaries between candidates, hiring managers, and panel members across multiple rounds of interviews.
processes.explanation: AI handles the entire scheduling dance — checks availability, proposes times, sends confirmations, and reschedules when people cancel. Zero email ping-pong.

processes.name: Client job intake and brief creation
processes.description: Meeting clients to understand the role, writing the job description, agreeing terms, and setting up the vacancy in your ATS.
processes.explanation: AI drafts the job description from intake meeting notes, suggests salary benchmarks from market data, and pre-populates the ATS setup. Consultants refine rather than create from scratch.

processes.name: Candidate status updates to clients
processes.description: Sending regular updates to clients on pipeline status, interview feedback, and candidate availability for each active role.
processes.explanation: AI generates pipeline reports and status updates from your ATS data, drafted in your firm's tone. Consultants review and send rather than compiling from multiple screens.

processes.name: Reference checking
processes.description: Contacting referees, asking structured questions, recording responses, and summarising findings for the client file.
processes.explanation: AI sends reference request forms, follows up automatically, and compiles responses into a structured report. Recruiters only get involved for verbal references or concerns.

processes.name: Compliance documentation (IR35, right-to-work)
processes.description: Collecting right-to-work documents, running IR35 assessments for contractors, and maintaining audit-ready records.
processes.explanation: AI guides candidates through document submission, runs IR35 assessments using HMRC's CEST criteria, and flags compliance gaps before they become problems.

processes.name: Placement invoicing and fee tracking
processes.description: Generating invoices when placements are made, tracking fee milestones and rebate periods, and chasing payment.
processes.explanation: AI generates invoices automatically when placement status changes, monitors rebate periods, and sends payment reminders on schedule. Finance admin drops dramatically.

processes.name: Database hygiene and candidate re-engagement
processes.description: Cleaning stale records in your ATS/CRM, re-engaging dormant candidates, and ensuring contact details are current.
processes.explanation: AI identifies stale records, sends re-engagement campaigns to dormant candidates, and updates records based on responses. Your database becomes an asset rather than a liability.

processes.name: Weekly and monthly performance reporting
processes.description: Pulling KPIs (placements, fees, time-to-fill, conversion rates) from your ATS for team meetings and client SLA reporting.
processes.explanation: AI generates reports automatically from your ATS data, highlights trends and anomalies, and drafts commentary. What took a Friday afternoon happens in minutes.

processes.name: Monthly performance reporting for retainer clients
processes.description: Pulling analytics from Google, Meta, LinkedIn, compiling results against KPIs, writing commentary, and formatting client-ready reports.
processes.explanation: AI pulls data from all platforms via APIs, identifies significant movements, and drafts the commentary. What took a full day per client becomes a 30-minute review and send.

processes.name: Social media content scheduling
processes.description: Planning, writing, and scheduling posts across multiple platforms for multiple clients — maintaining brand voice and posting frequency.
processes.explanation: AI generates post drafts from campaign briefs and brand guides, adapts copy per platform, and suggests optimal posting times. Content managers curate and approve rather than write from zero.

processes.name: Client brief and feedback management
processes.description: Receiving client briefs, interpreting requirements, distributing to the creative team, and managing feedback rounds through to approval.
processes.explanation: AI structures incoming briefs into standardised formats, tracks feedback cycles, and flags when approvals are overdue. Less time lost to version confusion.

processes.name: Campaign performance analysis
processes.description: Analysing A/B test results, audience segments, creative variants, and attribution across channels to optimise spend.
processes.explanation: AI runs analysis automatically as data comes in, identifies winning variants, suggests budget reallocation, and spots diminishing returns. Strategists make decisions faster with better data.

processes.name: Competitive research and trend monitoring
processes.description: Tracking competitor campaigns, creative approaches, messaging, and market positioning to inform client strategy.
processes.explanation: AI monitors competitor channels, flags new campaigns or messaging shifts, and produces regular competitive intelligence summaries. Strategy stays current without manual trawling.

processes.name: Proposal and pitch deck creation
processes.description: Writing creative proposals for new business pitches — case studies, approach, team bios, pricing, timelines, and mock-ups.
processes.explanation: AI assembles first drafts from your case study library and proposal templates, personalised to the prospect's industry and brief. Creative directors focus on the killer idea, not the boilerplate.

processes.name: Invoice and timesheet management
processes.description: Tracking time across multiple client projects, reconciling against retainer hours or budgets, generating invoices, and chasing payment.
processes.explanation: AI tracks time from project management tools, alerts when budgets are running hot, generates invoices on schedule, and sends payment reminders. Finance overhead drops to near-zero.

processes.name: SEO content planning and keyword research
processes.description: Researching keywords, analysing search intent, mapping content gaps, and building editorial calendars for client SEO strategy.
processes.explanation: AI analyses search data, identifies content opportunities with realistic ranking potential, and drafts content briefs with target keywords, structure, and competitive benchmarks.

processes.name: Asset management and file organisation
processes.description: Organising creative assets (logos, photos, videos, fonts, brand guidelines) across clients, ensuring teams find the right files.
processes.explanation: AI auto-tags and categorises assets, enforces naming conventions, flags outdated files, and surfaces relevant assets when new projects start.

processes.name: Email campaign setup and testing
processes.description: Building email campaigns — HTML templates, merge tags, segmentation, A/B variants, scheduling, and pre-send testing across email clients.
processes.explanation: AI generates email copy from campaign briefs, builds HTML templates, sets up segmentation rules, and runs pre-send checks for rendering issues. Marketers focus on strategy, not platform mechanics.
